fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: resolve_dep
fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve a dependency string to a Cargo.toml dependency value.

Logic:
1. If `value` is a path that exists on disk → `{ path = "..." }`
2. If `version_override` is set → `"<version>"`
3. Check crates.io for `value` → if found, use latest version
4. Warn and fall back to `{ path = "<value>" }`

fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: check_crates_io
fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.doc_comment: Check crates.io for a crate and return its latest version, if found.

fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: init_interface
fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.signature:
(
    name: &str,
    trait_name: &str,
    path: Option<&Path>,
    version: Option<&str>,
) -> Result

fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.name: init_plugin
fidius-cli/src/commands.rs.signature:
(
    name: &str,
    interface: &str,
    trait_name: &str,
    path: Option<&Path>,
    version: Option<&str>,
) -> Result

fidius-cli/src/main.rs.name: Commands
fidius-cli/src/main.rs.signature: InitInterface | InitPlugin | Keygen | Sign | Verify | Inspect | Package

fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.name: fides_cmd
fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.doc_comment:
Everything is generated from scratch by the CLI. No pre-written fixtures.

True end-to-end test: scaffold → package → build → sign → load → call.

fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.name: workspace_fidius_path
fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.doc_comment: Path to the workspace root's `fidius` facade crate (for local dep resolution).

fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.name: full_pipeline_scaffold_package_build_sign_load_call
fidius-cli/tests/full_pipeline.rs.doc_comment:
Everything is generated from scratch by the CLI. No pre-written fixtures.

True end-to-end test: scaffold → package → build → sign → load → call.

fidius-core/src/async_runtime.rs.name: FIDIUS_RUNTIME
fidius-core/src/async_runtime.rs.doc_comment:
The shared tokio runtime for this dylib.

Initialized on first use. One runtime per dylib, shared across all
plugin implementations.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: FIDIUS_MAGIC
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment: Magic bytes identifying a Fidius plugin registry.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: REGISTRY_VERSION
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment: Current version of the `PluginRegistry` struct layout.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: ABI_VERSION
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Current version of the `PluginDescriptor` struct layout.
Bumped to 2 to add `method_count` field.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: BufferStrategyKind
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Buffer management strategy for an interface.

Selected per-trait via `#[plugin_interface(buffer = ...)]`.
Determines the FFI function pointer signatures in the vtable.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: WireFormat
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Wire serialization format.

Determined at compile time via `cfg(debug_assertions)`.
Host rejects plugins compiled with a mismatched format.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: BufferStrategyKind
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: fmt
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: fmt
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.signature: { magic: [u8; 8], registry_version: u32, plugin_count: u32, descriptors: *const *const PluginDescriptor }
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Top-level registry exported by every Fidius plugin dylib.

Each dylib exports exactly one `FIDIUS_PLUGIN_REGISTRY` static symbol
pointing to this struct. The registry contains pointers to one or more
`PluginDescriptor`s (supporting multiple plugins per dylib).

# Safety

- `descriptors` must point to a valid array of `plugin_count` pointers.
- Each pointer in the array must point to a valid `PluginDescriptor`.
- All pointed-to data must have `'static` lifetime (typically link-time constants).

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginRegistry
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginDescriptor
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.signature: { abi_version: u32, interface_name: *const c_char, interface_hash: u64, interface_version: u32, capabilities: u64, wire_...
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Metadata descriptor for a single plugin within a dylib.

Contains all information the host needs to validate and call the plugin
without executing any plugin code. All string fields are pointers to
static, null-terminated C strings embedded in the dylib.

# Safety

- `interface_name` and `plugin_name` must point to valid, null-terminated,
UTF-8 C strings with `'static` lifetime.
- `vtable` must point to a valid `#[repr(C)]` vtable struct matching the
interface identified by `interface_name` and `interface_hash`.
- When `buffer_strategy == PluginAllocated`, `free_buffer` must be `Some`.
- All pointed-to data must outlive any `PluginHandle` derived from this descriptor.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginDescriptor
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: PluginDescriptor
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.

FFI descriptor and registry types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: DescriptorPtr
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
A `Sync` wrapper for a raw pointer to a `PluginDescriptor`.

Used in static contexts where a `*const PluginDescriptor` needs to live
in a `static` variable (which requires `Sync`). The pointed-to descriptor
must have `'static` lifetime.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: DescriptorPtr
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
All types use `#[repr(C)]` layout and are read directly from dylib memory.
These types form the stable C ABI contract between host and plugin.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: interface_name_str
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Read the `interface_name` field as a Rust `&str`.

# Safety

`interface_name` must point to a valid, null-terminated, UTF-8 C string
that outlives the returned reference.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: plugin_name_str
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Read the `plugin_name` field as a Rust `&str`.

# Safety

`plugin_name` must point to a valid, null-terminated, UTF-8 C string
that outlives the returned reference.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: buffer_strategy_kind
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the `buffer_strategy` field as a `BufferStrategyKind`.

Returns `Err(value)` if the discriminant is unknown. This can happen
with malformed plugins — callers should reject rather than panic.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: wire_format_kind
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the `wire_format` field as a `WireFormat`.

Returns `Err(value)` if the discriminant is unknown.

fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.name: has_capability
fidius-core/src/descriptor.rs.doc_comment:
Check if the given optional method capability bit is set.

Returns `false` for bit indices >= 64 rather than panicking.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
fidius-core/src/error.rs.signature: { code: String, message: String, details: Option<String> }
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Error returned by plugin method implementations to signal business logic failures.

Serialized across the FFI boundary via the wire format. The host deserializes
this from the output buffer when the FFI shim returns `STATUS_PLUGIN_ERROR`.

The `details` field is stored as a JSON string (not `serde_json::Value`)
so that it serializes correctly under both JSON and bincode wire formats.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: new
fidius-core/src/error.rs.signature: (code: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: with_details
fidius-core/src/error.rs.signature:
(
        code: impl Into<String>,
        message: impl Into<String>,
        details: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Self
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new `PluginError` with structured details.

The `serde_json::Value` is serialized to a JSON string for storage.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: details_value
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Parse the `details` field back into a `serde_json::Value`.

Returns `None` if details is absent or fails to parse.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: fmt
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/error.rs.name: PluginError
fidius-core/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Error types for the Fidius plugin framework.

fidius-core/src/hash.rs.name: fnv1a
fidius-core/src/hash.rs.doc_comment: Compute the FNV-1a 64-bit hash of a byte slice.

fidius-core/src/hash.rs.name: interface_hash
fidius-core/src/hash.rs.doc_comment:
Compute the interface hash from a set of method signatures.

Signatures are sorted lexicographically before hashing to ensure
order-independence. Each signature is joined with `\n` as a separator.

This function is **not** `const` because it allocates for sorting.
The proc macro calls this at compile time via a build-script-like pattern,
or uses `fnv1a` directly on pre-sorted, concatenated signatures.

fidius-core/src/hash.rs.doc_comment:

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageManifest
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
A parsed package manifest, generic over the host-defined metadata schema.

The `M` type parameter is the host's metadata schema. If the `[metadata]`
section of `package.toml` doesn't deserialize into `M`, parsing fails —
this is how schema validation works.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageHeader
fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature: { name: String, version: String, interface: String, interface_version: u32 }
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Fixed header fields that every package manifest must have.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: PackageError
fidius-core/src/package.rs.signature: ManifestNotFound | ParseError | Io | BuildFailed | SignatureNotFound | SignatureInvalid
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Errors that can occur when loading a package manifest.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: load_manifest
load_manifest_untyped
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Load a manifest validating only the fixed header (accepting any metadata).

Uses `toml::Value` as the metadata type so any `[metadata]` section is accepted.
Useful for CLI tools that validate structure without knowing the host's schema.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: package_digest
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Compute a deterministic SHA-256 digest over all package source files.

Walks the package directory, collects all files (excluding `target/`,
`.git/`, and `*.sig` files), sorts by relative path, and feeds each
file's relative path and contents into a SHA-256 hasher.

The resulting 32-byte digest covers the entire package contents.
Sign this digest to protect against tampering.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: collect_files
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment: Recursively collect file paths relative to `root`, skipping excluded dirs/files.

fidius-core/src/package.rs.name: tests
fidius-core/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
host-defined schema type.
an extensible `[metadata]` section validated via serde against a
manifest. The manifest has a fixed header (name, version, interface) and
A package is a directory containing plugin source code and a `package.toml`

Source package manifest types and parsing.

fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: DescriptorEntry
fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: A submitted descriptor pointer. Used with `inventory` for distributed collection.

fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: build_registry
fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Build the plugin registry from all submitted descriptors.

Allocates a `Vec` of descriptor pointers and leaks it to get a `'static` pointer.
Called once; the result is cached in `OnceLock`.

fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: get_registry
fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Get or build the plugin registry.

Returns a `'static` reference to the registry. Built on first call,
cached for subsequent calls.

fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: REGISTRY
fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
`fidius_get_registry` export function that the host calls via `dlsym`.
Plugin crates call `fidius_plugin_registry!()` once in their lib.rs to emit the
Each `#[plugin_impl]` submits its `PluginDescriptor` pointer via `inventory::submit!`.

Plugin registry assembly for multi-plugin dylibs.

fidius-core/src/registry.rs.name: fidius_plugin_registry
fidius-core/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Emit the `fidius_get_registry` export function.

Call this once in your plugin cdylib's `lib.rs`. The host calls
`dlsym("fidius_get_registry")` and invokes it to get the registry.

```ignore
fidius::fidius_plugin_registry!();
```

fidius-core/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_OK
fidius-core/src/status.rs.doc_comment: Method executed successfully. Output buffer contains the serialized result.

fidius-core/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL
fidius-core/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
Output buffer was too small (CallerAllocated/Arena strategies only).
The `out_len` parameter contains the required size. Retry with a larger buffer.

fidius-core/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_SERIALIZATION_ERROR
fidius-core/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
Serialization or deserialization failed at the FFI boundary.
This indicates a bug in the generated shims or a type mismatch.

fidius-core/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_PLUGIN_ERROR
fidius-core/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
The plugin method returned an error. The output buffer contains a
serialized `PluginError` with details.

fidius-core/src/status.rs.name: STATUS_PANIC
fidius-core/src/status.rs.doc_comment:
A panic was caught at the `extern "C"` boundary via `catch_unwind`.
The output buffer may contain a panic message string, but this is not guaranteed.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: WIRE_FORMAT
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: The wire format active in this build.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: WIRE_FORMAT
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: The wire format active in this build.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: WireError
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment: Errors that can occur during wire serialization or deserialization.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: serialize
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
Serialize a value using the active wire format.

Returns JSON bytes in debug builds, bincode bytes in release builds.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: deserialize
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
Deserialize a value from the active wire format.

Expects JSON bytes in debug builds, bincode bytes in release builds.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: serialize
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
Serialize a value using the active wire format.

Returns JSON bytes in debug builds, bincode bytes in release builds.

fidius-core/src/wire.rs.name: deserialize
fidius-core/src/wire.rs.doc_comment:
Deserialize a value from the active wire format.

Expects JSON bytes in debug builds, bincode bytes in release builds.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: FfiFn
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.signature: = unsafe extern "C" fn(*const u8, u32, *mut *mut u8, *mut u32) -> i32
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Type alias for the PluginAllocated FFI function pointer signature.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.signature: { _library: Arc<Library>, vtable: *const c_void, free_buffer: Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut u8, usize)>, capabilities...
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
A handle to a loaded plugin, ready for calling methods.

Holds an `Arc<Library>` to keep the dylib loaded as long as any handle exists.
Call methods via `call_method()` which handles serialization, FFI, and cleanup.

`PluginHandle` is `Send + Sync`. Plugin methods take `&self` (enforced by
the macro), so concurrent calls from multiple threads are safe as long as
the plugin implementation is thread-safe internally.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: PluginHandle — type-safe proxy for calling plugin methods via FFI.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: PluginHandle — type-safe proxy for calling plugin methods via FFI.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: PluginHandle
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: PluginHandle — type-safe proxy for calling plugin methods via FFI.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: new
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.signature:
(
        library: Arc<Library>,
        vtable: *const c_void,
        free_buffer: Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(*mut u8, usize)>,
        capabilities: u64,
        method_count: u32,
        info: PluginInfo,
    ) -> Self
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Create a new PluginHandle. Crate-private — use `from_loaded()` instead.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: from_loaded
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment: Create a PluginHandle from a LoadedPlugin.

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: call_method
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Call a plugin method by vtable index.

Serializes the input, calls the FFI function pointer at the given index,
checks the status code, deserializes the output, and frees the plugin-allocated buffer.

# Arguments
* `index` - The method index in the vtable (0-based, in declaration order)
* `input` - The input argument to serialize and pass to the plugin

fidius-host/src/handle.rs.name: has_capability
fidius-host/src/handle.rs.doc_comment:
Check if an optional method is supported (capability bit is set).

Returns `false` for bit indices >= 64 rather than panicking.

and returns metadata for all valid plugins found.

with the given name. Returns the loaded plugin ready for calling.

fidius-host/src/loader.rs.doc_comment: Validate a single descriptor and copy to owned types.

fidius-host/src/loader.rs.signature:
(
    plugin: &LoadedPlugin,
    expected_hash: Option<u64>,
    expected_wire: Option<WireFormat>,
    expected_strategy: Option<BufferStrategyKind>,
) -> Result<(), LoadError>
fidius-host/src/loader.rs.doc_comment: Validate a loaded plugin against expected interface parameters.

fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: load_package_manifest
fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Load and validate a package manifest against a host-defined schema.

This is the primary entry point for host applications working with packages.
The type parameter `M` is the host's metadata schema — if the manifest's
`[metadata]` section doesn't deserialize into `M`, this returns an error.

# Example

```ignore
#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct MySchema {
category: String,
min_host_version: String,
}

let manifest = load_package_manifest::<MySchema>(Path::new("./packages/blur/"))?;
assert_eq!(manifest.metadata.category, "image-processing");
```

fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: discover_packages
fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Discover packages in a directory.

Scans `dir` for subdirectories containing a `package.toml` file.
Returns the paths to each package directory found.

fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: verify_package
fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Verify a source package's signature against trusted public keys.

Recomputes the package digest from files on disk and verifies the
`package.sig` signature against the provided trusted keys.

# Errors

- `PackageError::SignatureNotFound` — if `package.sig` doesn't exist
- `PackageError::SignatureInvalid` — if no trusted key verifies the signature

fidius-host/src/package.rs.name: build_package
fidius-host/src/package.rs.doc_comment:
Build a package by running `cargo build` inside the package directory.

Returns the path to the compiled cdylib on success.

fidius-host/src/signing.rs.name: sig_path_for
fidius-host/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Compute the detached signature file path for a given file.

Appends `.sig` to the full filename (e.g., `foo.dylib` → `foo.dylib.sig`).

fidius-host/src/signing.rs.name: verify_signature
fidius-host/src/signing.rs.doc_comment:
Verify a plugin dylib's signature against trusted public keys.

Reads the dylib bytes and the detached `.sig` file, then verifies
the Ed25519 signature against each trusted key until one matches.

# Errors

- `LoadError::SignatureRequired` — if the `.sig` file doesn't exist
- `LoadError::SignatureInvalid` — if no trusted key verifies the signature
